purpose: Demand-led candidate books to evaluate when CrowdListen surfaces source gaps.

candidates.title: Obviously Awesome
candidates.author: April Dunford
candidates.reason: Useful for recurring questions about positioning, differentiation, and explaining why a product matters.

candidates.title: Crossing the Chasm
candidates.author: Geoffrey A. Moore
candidates.reason: Useful for questions about early adopters, beachhead markets, and moving from interest to adoption.

candidates.title: Traction
candidates.author: Gabriel Weinberg and Justin Mares
candidates.reason: Useful for demand questions about channels, repeatable growth tests, and where to find users.

candidates.title: Competing Against Luck
candidates.author: Clayton M. Christensen, Taddy Hall, Karen Dillon, and David S. Duncan
candidates.reason: Useful for translating messy customer language into demand, jobs, and buying triggers.
